issue: Universality of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons

entries.position_summary: Supports fair, objective, and impartial IAEA safeguards, urging universal adoption of comprehensive safeguards agreements and encouraging states to voluntarily join the Additional Protocol. Calls for states outside the NPT to accede as non-nuclear-weapon states and place all nuclear facilities under IAEA safeguards, emphasizing comprehensive and balanced implementation of NPT obligations.
entries.quote: Implement NPT obligations in a comprehensive, faithful and balanced manner. Support the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in fulfilling its safeguard mandate in a fair, objective and impartial manner. Promote the universality of the IAEA comprehensive safeguards agreement, and encourage relevant States to voluntarily join the Additional Protocols. States that have not yet joined the NPT should do so as non-nuclear-weapon States as soon as possible, and place all nuclear facilities under the safeguards of the IAEA.

entries.position_summary: The country urges comprehensive and balanced compliance with the NPT, supports the IAEA’s fair and impartial safeguards, and advocates universal adoption of comprehensive safeguards agreements and the Additional Protocol. It calls on non-parties to join the NPT as non-nuclear-weapon states and place all nuclear facilities under IAEA safeguards.
entries.quote: 12. All parties should comply with the NPT obligations in a comprehensive, faithful and balanced manner,support the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in fulfilling its safeguard function in a fair, objective and impartial manner, and promote the universality of the IAEA comprehensive safeguards agreements and additional protocols. States that have not yet joined the NPT should do so as non-nuclear-weapon States as soon as possible, and place all nuclear facilities under the safeguards of IAEA in accordance with the NPT provisions.

entries.position_summary: The country urges full implementation of the 1995 NPT Middle East resolution and calls for the early establishment of a Middle East zone free of nuclear and other WMD, with all regional states and NWS (including an NPT depository) participating in related UNGA-led conferences. It presses Israel to join the NPT as a non-nuclear-weapon state and place all its nuclear facilities under comprehensive IAEA safeguards.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, Over the recent years, the security situation of the Middle East has deteriorated seriously, the aspiration of Middle East people to be free from nuclear weapons is urgent. While promoting reconciliation and cooperation among countries in the Middle East and advancing the peace process of the Middle East, the international community should fully accommodate legitimate security concerns of the countries in Middle East,actively implement the resolution on the Middle East of the1995 NPT review and extension conference, and continue to make efforts for the early establishment of a Middle East Zone Free of Nuclear Weapons and Other Weapons of Mass Destruction. Regional states, nuclear-weapon States especially one of the depositories of the NPT should actively implement the relevant UNGA resolutions, attend the international Conference on Establishing a Middle East Zone Free of Nuclear Weapons and other Weapons of Mass Destruction. Israel should join the NPT as a non-nuclear-weapon State as soon as possible and place all its nuclear facilities under the safeguards of the IAEA.

entries.position_summary: The country urges universality of the NPT by calling on non-parties to join as non-nuclear-weapon states and place all facilities under IAEA safeguards. It supports strengthening IAEA safeguards, including universalizing comprehensive safeguards agreements and additional protocols. It advocates establishing a Middle East WMD-free zone and calls on nuclear-weapon states to respect and join protocols to nuclear-weapon-free zones, noting willingness to sign the SEANWFZ Protocol.
entries.quote: First, it is imperative to be staunch defenders of the cause of nuclear non-proliferation. States parties to the NPT should implement their commitments of nuclear non-proliferation. States that are not party to the NPT should join the Treaty as non-nuclear-weapon States as soon as possible, and place all of their nuclear facilities under the safeguards of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). The international community should support efforts to establish a Middle East Zone Free of Nuclear Weapons and Other Weapons of Mass Destruction. Regional States, nuclear-weapon States especially the depositories of the NPT should take an active part in the international Conference on Establishing a Middle East Zone Free of Nuclear Weapons and other Weapons of Mass Destruction that started from 2019. Nuclear-weapon States should respect the legal status of nuclear-weapon-free zones, sign and ratify as soon as possible all additional protocols related to treaties on nuclear-weapon-free zones open for signatures. China is willing to take the lead in signing the Protocol to the Treaty on the Southeast Asia Nuclear-Weapon-Free Zone. The international community should improve the universality of the IAEA comprehensive safeguards agreements and additional protocols, and support the IAEA in enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of IAEA safeguards system following the

entries.position_summary: The paragraph supports a forward- and backward-looking NPT review process that evaluates implementation and identifies paths for further progress. It also emphasizes strengthening implementation of the Treaty and achieving its universality.
entries.quote: NPT/CONF.2026/PC.II/WP.4 Proliferation Treaty Review and Extension Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons decided that “Review Conferences should look forward as well as back. They should evaluate the results of the period they are reviewing, including the implementation of undertakings of the States parties under the Treaty, and identify the areas in which, and the means through which, further progress should be sought in the future. Review Conferences should also address specifically what might be done to strengthen the implementation of the Treaty and to achieve its universality”.

entries.position_summary: China, as incoming P5 coordinator, pledges to enhance P5 communication and cooperation to uphold the NPT, seeks productive outcomes from the current review cycle, and reaffirms support for the NPT’s universality, authority, and effectiveness.
entries.quote: China will soon take over as the P5 coordinator, and is willing to enhance the P5’s communication and cooperation on issues of upholding the NPT regime. We are also willing to work for fruitful outcomes of this round of the review process, and to jointly uphold the universality, authority and effectiveness of the NPT.

entries.position_summary: The statement highlights that the 1995 decision expanded the PrepCom’s mandate and time, including consideration of universality, but notes that efforts to produce substantive recommendations have consistently failed. It attributes this to the consensus rule and the practice that nothing is agreed until everything is agreed, which would require pre-negotiating compromises a year before the Review Conference.
entries.quote: 11. Until the 1995 Review and Extension Conference, Preparatory Committee meetings focused on administrative matters and making the necessary decisions and arrangements for the Review Conference. The 1995 decision on strengthening the review process gave the Preparatory Committee more time (three meetings vs one) and a much broader mandate “to consider principles, objectives and ways in order to promote the full implementation of the Treaty, as well as its universality, and to make recommendations thereon to the Review Conference”. Since then, the Chairs of the Preparatory Committee, particularly at its third session, have sought to build consensus on substantive recommendations to the Review Conference. However, this ambition has never been achieved. Reaching consensus at review conferences has proven to be difficult enough, often hinging on key compromises sought during the final week of the Conference. And since many States parties adopt the approach that “nothing is agreed until everything is agreed”, making substantive recommendations would essentially require pre-negotiating such compromises a full year in advance.

entries.position_summary: Calls for continued, in-depth discussions to strengthen the NPT review process, noting the failure to agree reflects the complexity of challenges across the Treaty’s three pillars. Affirms the NPT’s irreplaceable status and urges all States Parties to uphold its object and purpose while strengthening its authority, effectiveness, and universality.
entries.quote: Through the Working Group’s work in the past five days, I believe that all parties have understood better that various positions and proposals on strengthening the treaty review process need to be explored further in future’s review process. That the parties failed to reach an agreement is not only the result of different perspectives in observing and understanding international situation, differences in the positions and ideas, still less political will. More importantly, many challenges facing the NPT’s three pillars are in themselves diverse, complex, and unprecedented. Progress in strengthening the review process will only be possible on the premise of continuing in-depth discussions on all relevant issues. Standing at the new starting point of the NPT review process, States parties should agree on at least the following three points: First, the status and role of the NPT regime is irreplaceable. All States parties should work together to uphold and strengthen the authority, effectiveness and universality of the NPT. Second, the object and purpose of the NPT must be upheld.

entries.position_summary: The country calls for balanced advancement of all three NPT pillars, emphasizes maintaining the NPT’s authority and universality, and urges all non-parties to join as non-nuclear-weapon states. It supports eliminating the risk of nuclear war and sustained efforts toward a world free of nuclear weapons.
entries.quote: Advance the three pillars of nuclear disarmament, nuclear non-proliferation and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in a balanced way, maintain the authority, effectiveness and universality of the NPT, consolidate the international non-proliferation regime with the NPT as its cornerstone, eliminate the risk of nuclear war, make unremitting efforts to achieve a world free of nuclear weapons. States that have not yet joined the NPT should do so as non-nuclear-weapon States as soon as possible.

entries.position_summary: China advocates a cooperative, indivisible security approach and commits to dialogue to advance the NPT review process. It supports strengthening the NPT’s universality, authority, and effectiveness.
entries.quote: Last year, Chinese President Xi Jinping proposed a Global Security Initiative at the opening ceremony of the Boao Forum. He stressed the importance of upholding a vision of common, comprehensive, cooperative and sustainable security.An enduring solution to global security challenges lies in upholding the principle of indivisible security, taking each other’s legitimate security concerns seriously, and building a balanced, effective and sustainable security architecture with a view to universal and common security.The ChinaArms Control and DisarmamentAssociation is willing to continue to strengthen dialogue and cooperation with all parties, push for progress in the new round of the NPT review process, continuously strengthen the universality, authority and effectiveness of the NPT, and make new contributions to promoting peace, security anddevelopment oftheworld.

entries.position_summary: China expresses support for strengthening the NPT’s universality, authority, and effectiveness, and is willing to work with all parties to that end. It frames this as part of contributing to international peace, security, and development.
entries.quote: China is always a builder of world peace, a contributor to global development and a defender of international order. China is willing to work with all parties to strengthen the universality, authority and effectiveness of NPT, and to address the complex and intertwined challenges of international security and development with a win-win mindset, in order to pass on the torch of peace from generation to generation, sustain development and make civilization flourish, and inject more stability and certainty into the turbulent and changing times.
